title: SpaceInvite code comment claims 80-bit entropy but is actually 40 bits
description: In jmap.rs, the SpaceInvite code generation comment states '80-bit random' but the implementation takes id.to_string()[10..18] — 8 Crockford Base32 characters from a ULID. Each Base32 character encodes 5 bits, so 8 chars = 40 bits of entropy, not 80 bits. 40-bit invite codes are guessable via brute force if codes are not rate-limited. Fix: either update the comment to reflect 40-bit reality and separately evaluate whether 40-bit codes are acceptable given the threat model, or generate a proper 80-bit (16 Base32 char) random code independent of the ULID.
